mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC falls below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce corporate earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE rises above the 0.2pp threshold, or the signal out-of-sample Sharpe ratio is negative.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand growth that drives corporate revenue acceleration, yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative 60-day forward returns in over 60% of triggered months, or the interaction term loses statistical significance with a p-value above 0.10 in out-of-sample regression tests.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the index of industrial production during a late-cycle expansion phase reflects capacity constraints and supply-side bottlenecks that trigger short-term equity sell-offs, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive or flat during late-cycle INDPRO contractions, or the mechanism fails to generate a negative Rank IC in late-cycle subsamples.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate level exceeding 4.0 percent combined with a positive month-over-month change indicates aggressive monetary tightening that compresses equity multiples and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of observations meeting the trigger condition, or the signal predictive accuracy drops below random chance in tightening regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic activity and rising household income, which supports corporate earnings growth and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns show a negative or zero correlation with PAYEMS growth over any 5-year rolling window, or the signal out-of-sample R-squared falls below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising consumer price inflation and stagnant real GDP growth indicates stagflationary pressure that erodes real corporate margins, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The CPIAUCSL-GDPC1 divergence fails to predict negative 20-day returns, or the interaction effect reverses sign in out-of-sample testing with a p-value above 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: An acceleration in industrial production during a post-shock recovery phase signals rapidly improving supply-side capacity and easing input constraints, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 55% of post-shock recovery periods with accelerating INDPRO, or the signal cumulative abnormal return is statistically indistinguishable from zero.
